# contextlake

> A local context layer for your AI tools: mirror your Git repositories, index them into a
> queryable knowledge graph + wiki, and serve it to AI editors over MCP. Offline-first, so
> agents answer from real source instead of guessing. Python CLI, published on PyPI.

## Get started

- [Overview](https://sayak.in/contextlake/docs.html): A local context layer for your AI tools: mirror your repos, index them into a knowledge graph, and serve it over MCP.
- [Quickstart](https://sayak.in/contextlake/quickstart.html): Install, bootstrap, and wire your editor, the whole Mirror -> Knowledge -> Serve path in a few minutes.

## Build your knowledge base

- [Index the code graph](https://sayak.in/contextlake/index-code-graph.html): Turn your mirrored repos into a queryable code graph: incremental indexing, and the full node and edge model across 14 languages, Terraform, SQL, and web topology.
- [Connect and enrich](https://sayak.in/contextlake/connect-enrich.html): Link repos to their issues, docs, and designs, manage sources, and pull grounded external facts into the knowledge layer with query-driven enrichment.
- [Semantic search](https://sayak.in/contextlake/semantic-search.html): Natural-language and hybrid graph-propagation retrieval: embed your code, tune the vector backend, and query across repos and languages.
- [Generate the wiki](https://sayak.in/contextlake/generate-wiki.html): Turn the graph into grounded, council-verified prose per repo: searchable, enrichment-aware, with a provenance footer.
- [Model providers](https://sayak.in/contextlake/model-providers.html): The pluggable embeddings and wiki backends: auto, built-in CPU, Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, and agent-CLI, with data-sharing posture and setup.

## Using contextlake

- [Usage & config](https://sayak.in/contextlake/usage.html): Every mirror command, configuration, branch safety, and scheduling, the reference for the Mirror layer.
- [Knowledge layer](https://sayak.in/contextlake/knowledge-layer.html): Turn the mirror into a queryable graph with search, a wiki, and connectors.
- [Dashboard](https://sayak.in/contextlake/dashboard.html): A guided tour of the local, offline-first dashboard: the fleet overview, per-repo anatomy, the architecture graph, blast radius, and generating a wiki.
- [Serve (MCP)](https://sayak.in/contextlake/serve.html): Expose the knowledge layer over MCP and wire your editors (Claude Code, Windsurf, Kiro) in one command.
- [Visualize the graph](https://sayak.in/contextlake/visualize.html): Draw bounded, offline graph slices (`contextlake graph`) in HTML, DOT, Mermaid, or a class diagram, plus the composed namespace C4 diagram.
- [Ownership and SMEs](https://sayak.in/contextlake/ownership.html): Find who owns a repo or path and who to ask, ranked recency-weighted from git history (`contextlake owners` / `who_knows`), no config or index required.
- [Benchmarks](https://sayak.in/contextlake/benchmarks.html): An honest, measured look at the token, cost, and correctness impact of connecting the contextlake MCP to your AI coding tools, new-code grounding first, plus search, maintenance, and the caveats.

## Under the hood

- [Architecture](https://sayak.in/contextlake/internals.html): How all three layers work inside, the store, concurrency, branch selection, extraction, and the offline boundary.
- [Storage](https://sayak.in/contextlake/storage.html): Where contextlake keeps everything it generates, one store directory, never polluting your synced repos.

## Writing style

- [Writing style](https://sayak.in/contextlake/style-guide.html): The spirit, the checklist, and links to the focused pages: voice, structure, formatting, and the word reference.
- [Voice and tone](https://sayak.in/contextlake/style-guide-voice.html): Second person, present tense, warm and grounded: the voice defaults, word choice, and writing for every reader.
- [Page types and structure](https://sayak.in/contextlake/style-guide-structure.html): The concept, how-to, reference, and tutorial page types, each with a fixed skeleton, and how to structure a page.
- [Formatting](https://sayak.in/contextlake/style-guide-formatting.html): Headings, lists, code, callouts, links, accessibility, and inclusive language: the mechanics that keep every page consistent.
- [Word reference](https://sayak.in/contextlake/style-guide-reference.html): The house-style decision cache, before and after rewrites, and the A-to-Z term reference.

## Brand

- [Brand overview](https://sayak.in/contextlake/brand.html): contextlake's brand in one page: essence, voice, the lake metaphor, Pebble, the palette, and the mark, with the full spec linked.

## Reference

- [Changelog](https://sayak.in/contextlake/changelog.html): Release history for contextlake.
